alerts.title: Unidentified UPI Transaction of ₹730
alerts.description: A single UPI transaction of ₹730 was made this month with an unclear merchant description ('You Have Done A Upi Txn. Check Detail'). This makes it impossible to verify the purpose or legitimacy of the payment.

alerts.title: Spending Jumped from ₹0 to ₹730 This Month
alerts.description: Last month's total spending was ₹0, while this month shows ₹730 entirely in the 'Others' category. Though the percentage change is not calculated, this represents a full spike from zero activity.

tips: Verify the ₹730 UPI transaction immediately — the merchant name 'You Have Done A Upi Txn. Check Detail' is a common pattern seen in phishing or mislabelled transactions. Check your UPI app (GPay/PhonePe/Paytm) for the actual payee name and UTR number.

All ₹730 of your spending this month is categorised under 'Others', which signals poor transaction categorisation. Link your bank account to a budgeting app like Walnut or Money Manager to auto-categorise future UPI payments.
Since your last month spending was ₹0 and this month is ₹730, consider setting a monthly UPI spend alert at ₹500 in your bank's mobile app to get notified before you cross your baseline.

Your 3-month average spend is ₹243.33/month, meaning this month's ₹730 is 3x your average. If this was a one-time payment (e.g., bill, fee), ensure it does not recur unknowingly by checking if any standing instruction or auto-debit was set up.

No subscriptions were detected this month, which is positive. However, given the unidentified ₹730 transaction, cross-check with your email inbox for any subscription confirmation receipts that may have triggered this charge.
